narrative:
Nathan Saliba is a central midfield player representing Canada. His overall rating of 7.1/10 places him among the top 6% of central midfields at this tournament. This rating reflects a blend of 44% club form and 56% from this World Cup.

Saliba has been remarkably productive in his 182 World Cup minutes, boasting 0.495 goals and an impressive 0.989 assists per 90 minutes. While his expected goals are 0.07 and expected assists are 0.208 per 90 minutes, his output is exceptional. He also contributes defensively with 2.967 tackles and 1.978 interceptions per 90 minutes.

Saliba is clearly a pivotal, all-action central midfielder for Canada, influencing both attack and defense. Scouts and fans should closely observe if he can maintain his exceptional goals and assists per 90 minutes. His ability to consistently impact games across all areas of the pitch will be key in Canada's remaining matches.
